message:
feat(modal): mouse hit context for prompts, paste support, focus-cycle wrap, snapshot refresh

## modal/dialog: prompt input gets first-class mouse + paste support

- New `DIALOG_HIT_INPUT` hit region (`HitRegion::Custom(1)`) lets the
  prompt input claim its own area in the hit map distinct from the
  buttons (`DIALOG_HIT_BUTTON`).
- `Dialog::handle_event` now branches on the hit region: when a left
  mouse-down lands on the prompt input, focus moves to the input,
  button focus is cleared, and any in-flight `pressed_button` is reset.
  When it lands on a button, the input is unfocused first so the next
  key event routes to the activated button rather than the input.
- New `Event::Paste` arm dispatches paste content into the prompt
  buffer via the new `handle_input_paste` helper, mirroring the
  per-key path but applied as a single text insertion.
- `cycle_focus` now wraps to the last button when reverse-cycling
  away from the input (was leaving `focused_button` as `None` and
  trapping focus on the input). Each cycle also resets
  `pressed_button` so a partially-pressed button does not stay
  visually depressed across focus moves.

## modal/stack: thread the rendered hit context through to children

- `StackModal::handle_event` now takes
  `hit: Option<(HitId, HitRegion, HitData)>` in addition to the
  stack-assigned `hit_id`. The stack passes through whatever the
  renderer last produced for the pointer location so child modals
  (notably the dialog above) can disambiguate input vs. button hits
  without re-running their own hit-test.
- `ModalStack::handle_event` itself takes the same optional hit and
  forwards it to the top modal's handler. The doc example was updated
  to pass `None` for keyboard-only callers.

## modal/focus_integration: align with the new signature

Updated to forward the optional hit through to the wrapped
`StackModal::handle_event` and to thread the corresponding parameters
through the focus-aware integration tests.

## crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/modal_e2e.rs

- `dialog_confirm_cancel_via_right_enter`: with the new wrap-on-cycle
  behavior, the focused button after one Right press is the OK button,
  not Cancel. Press Right twice to land on Cancel before pressing
  Enter, then assert the cancel result. Captures the new wrap intent.
- `modal_stack_lifo_escape_closes_top`: pass `None` as the hit context
  through `stack.handle_event(&press(KeyCode::Escape), None)`.
- `CountingModal::handle_event` updated to the new
  `(event, hit, hit_id)` signature.
- Imports `HitData` and `HitRegion` alongside `HitId` from
  `ftui_render::frame` so the new tuple type names resolve.

## Snapshot refresh

`dialog_alert_80x24.snap`, `dialog_confirm_80x24.snap`, and
`dialog_prompt_80x24.snap` updated to match the trimmed dialog body
height (the new clear paths in the widget hygiene work below shave
exactly one stale separator row from the rendered dialogs).
